items.title: Champions League recap: PSG, Atlético Madrid off to semifinals
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain and Atlético Madrid have advanced to the semifinals, following a day of UEFA Champions League quarterfinals action.

items.title: Barça out of UCL after rally vs. Atlético falls short
items.summary: Atlético Madrid held on after an early charge by Barcelona to reach the Champions League semifinals for the first time in nearly a decade on Tuesday.

items.title: Dembélé finishes Liverpool to put PSG in semis
items.summary: Ousmane Dembélé scored two late goals as Paris Saint-Germain extinguished Liverpool's hopes of a comeback to march into the Champions League semifinals 4-0 on aggregate.

items.title: Mascherano quits as Inter Miami head coach
items.summary: Javier Mascherano has quit as Inter Miami head coach, just four months after guiding the club to its first MLS Cup title.

items.title: Liverpool fear for 'very-bad' looking Ekitike injury
items.summary: Hugo Ekitike has handed a major injury worry to Liverpool and France after he was forced off in the first half of his club's Champions League quarterfinal loss to Paris Saint-Germain.

items.title: Slot fears Ekitike injury is 'really bad'
items.summary: Liverpool boss Arne Slot says Hugo Ekitike's injury 'looks really bad' after the France striker was taken off on a stretcher during Tuesday's Champions League loss to Paris St-Germain.

items.title: Flick tevreden ondanks uitschakeling: 'We spelen zoals Barça hoort te spelen'
items.summary: Hansi Flick kan moeilijk ontevreden zijn over zijn team na de nipte uitschakeling tegen Atlético Madrid in de kwartfinale van de Champions League. De trots overheerst direct na afloop bij trainer van Barcelona, die niettemin al ruimte zag om verbeterpunten aan te wijzen.

items.title: De Jong wijst na CL-uitschakeling naar pechfactor: 'We verdienden door te gaan'
items.summary: Frenkie de Jong baalt vanzelfsprekend als een stekker dat Barcelona uit de Champions League ligt . De technicus had zelf een beperkt aandeel, maar zag zijn team de overhand hebben in het tweeluik tegen Atlético Madrid.

items.title: Konaté heeft slecht gevoel over blessure Ekitiké: 'Ik bid voor hem'
items.summary: Dinsdagavond werden de eerste twee halve finalisten van de Champions League bekend: Paris Saint-Germain en Atlético Madrid schakelden respectievelijk Liverpool en Barcelona uit. Lees hieronder in de VI Live terug hoe de denderende kwartfinale-returns verliepen.

items.title: Slot zag Liverpool recht hebben op meer en baalt van arbitrage: 'Niet verbaasd'
items.summary: Dat de VAR dinsdagavond besloot om een aan Liverpool gegeven strafschop terug te draaien, verbaasde Arne Slot niet. Volgens de manager past het wel in het beeld van het seizoen van The Reds . Slot vond dat Liverpool meer verdiende tegen Paris Saint-Germain en houdt daarnaast zijn hart vast voor Hugo Ekitiké.

items.title: 'Als Liverpool vaker zo had gespeeld, dan had het meegedaan om de titel'
items.summary: De uitschakeling tegen Paris Saint-Germain kwam hard aan bij Liverpool, maar toch was er ergens ook berusting. Er was waardering voor de manier waarop de ploeg van Arne Slot zich na de wanprestatie in Parijs herpakte.

items.title: Wales must take care of own business - Wilkinson
items.summary: Rhian Wilkinson says Wales must "take care of our own business" before focusing on what looks set to be a key Women's World Cup qualifier against Czech Republic in June.

items.title: Arteta insists Arsenal must prove to themselves they have title mentality
items.summary: ‘We have to show on the pitch that we have the mindset’ Manager unsure when Saka will return from injury Mikel Arteta has admitted that Arsenal must prove to themselves that they have the right mindset to win the Premier League title but revealed there are major doubts over when Bukayo Saka will return from an achilles injury. The England forward will miss the second leg of Arsenal’s Champions League quarter-final against Sporting on Wednesday and looks set to still be sidelined for Sunday’s crucial Premier League showdown with Manchester City at the Etihad Stadium. Pep Guardiola said after City’s win over Chelsea last Sunday which cut Arsenal’s lead to six points that their recent resurgence is “about mindset, not tactics” as he attempts to win a seventh title in England. Continue reading...

items.title: Success over Spain - but how can England get best out of classy James?
items.summary: How can Sarina Wiegman get the best from one of England's most talented footballers, Lauren James?

items.title: Atlético hold off Barcelona comeback after Lookman strike and García red
items.summary: Defeat never tasted so good. At the end of a battle in which both teams had fought and both had bled too, a huge banner was unfurled at the south end of the Metropolitano and Atlético Madrid’s anthem belted out. “We give everything to win the cup,” it said, and, boy, had they. For the first time, Diego Simeone had seen his team lose a Champions League knockout game at home, but it didn’t matter. As that final whistle went, there was delirium, delight. They had waited a long time for this. Ten years and one day later, Atlético Madrid eliminated Barcelona to reach the semi-finals of the Champions League again. Continue reading...

items.title: McArdle praises NI's 'intent' in big win over Malta
items.summary: Northern Ireland boss Michael McArdle praises the "intent" his side showed in his first game in charge as they eased to a 4-0 win over Malta in World Cup qualifying.

items.title: Dembélé delivers knockout double as PSG end Liverpool’s European dream
items.summary: It felt routine in the end, the imperious champions of Europe through to another Champions League semi-final, Luis Enrique waving politely to the VIPs up in the Sir Kenny Dalglish stand having cavorted around Anfield following Paris Saint-Germain’s victory here last season, and a despondent Mohamed Salah bidding farewell to the Kop after his final European outing in a Liverpool shirt. But this was no routine departure from the Champions League for Liverpool. Having exited the FA Cup quarter-final 4-0 and with a whimper, Arne Slot’s side exited the Champions League quarter-final 4-0 on aggregate but with a fight. For 72 minutes they also had hope, went toe-to-toe with the finest unit in Europe and kept on pressing despite the loss of Hugo Ekitiké to a potentially serious injury and a debatable decision to give – and then take away – a penalty with the capacity to change everything. It will be of little consolation to Slot and his team that, for the second successive season against PSG, taking the fight to Luis Enrique’s champions and putting the fright on them brought no reward at Anfield. The damage inflicted in Paris last week proved irrepairable. Continue reading...

items.title: Hemp leads England past Spain to boost Women’s World Cup qualifying hopes
items.summary: World Cup qualifier: England 1-0 Spain (Hemp 3’) Lionesses win sides’ first meeting since Euros final A resolute England gave their chances of ­automatic qualification for 2027’s World Cup a ­tremendous boost as they beat the world champions, Spain, at Wembley and demonstrated they are strong enough defensively to hold off the most technically gifted squad in the women’s game. In a closely fought match in which both teams missed some ­gilt-edged opportunities, the sides were ­ultimately separated by two moments where the ball bounced extremely close to the line; one where it did cross the goalline and another where it did not. Continue reading...

items.title: West Brom could be hit with points deduction and relegation after season has ended
items.summary: EFL in race against time to hear charges Club alleged to have breached P&S rules West Brom could be given a points deduction that relegates them from the Championship after the season has finished as the club contest charges of breaching the English Football League’s profit and sustainability (P&S) rules. With the Championship league season concluding on 2 May the EFL is running out of time to hear the charges against West Brom, which relate to an alleged breach of the £39m loss limit in the three-year period culminating in the 2024-25 season. EFL sanctioning guidelines state that any punishment for a P&S breach must be applied in the campaign after it took place, which in West Brom’s case means this season, but the rulebook does not give a definitive cutoff point so it is unclear when the season ends. Continue reading...

items.title: Will Iraola's summer exit speed up managerial merry-go-round?
items.summary: Andoni Iraola's decision to leave Bournemouth could make it a frantic summer in the Premier League's managerial market, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

items.title: Ligue des Champions : le Barça passe tout près de l'exploit, Liverpool n'y aura pas cru
items.summary: On connaît les deux premiers qualifiés pour les demi-finales de la Ligue des Champions : l'Atlético Madrid et le PSG.

items.title: De eerste twee halvefinalisten in de Champions League zijn gekend
items.summary: Geen Remontada(s). Atlético Madrid en PSG wonnen de heenwedstrijden. En Atlético Madrid en PSG gaan ook simpelweg door naar de halve finales van de Champions League.

items.title: Bruges a-t-il identifié un filon ? Le Club aurait un oeil sur l'éventuel successeur de Tresoldi
items.summary: Le Club de Bruges sait que tôt ou tard, Nicolo Tresoldi s'envolera pour un grand club européen. Il faut donc déjà prévoir la question de sa succession.

items.title: Komt er een héél opvallende terugkeer aan? 'Kasper Dolberg is aangeboden bij RSC Anderlecht en... Club Brugge'
items.summary: Kasper Dolberg moet alweer vertrekken uit Amsterdam. En de geruchtenmolen draait op volle toeren. Zit er een héél spectaculaire terugkeer naar België aan te komen?

items.title: Le football français en deuil : le président de la FFF durant "l'affaire Knysna" est décédé à 90 ans
items.summary: Jean-Pierre Escalettes est décédé à l'âge de 90 ans, a annoncé la Fédération Française de Football.

items.title: 'Belgische eersteklasser wapent zich voor transferstrijd en verlengt contract van absolute sterkhouder'
items.summary: RAAL La Louvière heeft de optie in het contract van Sami Lahssaini gelicht. En dat hebben de groenhemden met voorbedachten rade gedaan.

items.title: Lieven Maesschalck, malgré lui au coeur de la tempête Lukaku : "Romelu en avait tout simplement marre"
items.summary: Romelu Lukaku a décidé de ne pas retourner en Italie pour poursuivre sa rééducation. Il a privilégié une revalidation chez sa vieille connaissance Lieven Maesschalck, qui se retrouve donc bien malgré lui au coeur des débats.

items.title: Woorden van Nicky Hayen keren als een boemerang terug: "Nog nooit gehoord van een trainer van een profclub"
items.summary: KRC Genk bleef afgelopen weekend steken op een scoreloos gelijkspel tegen OH Leuven. Nicky Hayen kwam met een héél opvallend excuus op de proppen. En die woorden keren als een boemerang terug.

items.title: Surprise en MLS : le coach de Messi et Suarez décide de s'en aller
items.summary: Javier Mascherano, coach de l'Inter Miami en MLS, quitte le club floridien. L'année passée, il avait remporté le premier titre de champion de l'histoire du club.

items.title: Manchester City, Manchester United én Arsenal willen Sandro Tonali: 'Newcastle United plakt dit héél duidelijke bedrag op Italiaan'
items.summary: Manchester City, Manchester United én Arsenal FC hebben eenzelfde speler in het vizier. En die speler mag bovendien vertrekken bij zijn huidige club. Al zal het wel een duit kosten.
